Q:
How can there be a Big Bang from nothing? The saying goes that you cannot create something from nothing. If that's the case, then there must be a creator.

Mar Moo:
[...] As for a creator being necessary, that’s an assumption out of ignorance. “I don’t know how it can happen naturally, so it must be magic”. I prefer “I don’t know how it can happen naturally, so let’s try to figure it out”, which is curiosity. I understand you like certainty, but that doesn’t make you right, it just means we don’t have all the answers yet. Go back in time, and look at how much of the world was unexplained. Now we know, why would I assume the rest can’t be explained? Why would you?
